extraction_policy.method: manual and model-assisted derived observation only
extraction_policy.automation_boundary: no automatic frame extraction, no copied screenshots, no source-layout cloning
extraction_policy.claim_boundary: these decompositions are production design contracts for the next rerun, not proof of public-video-grade output

visual_observations: A high-aesthetic opening uses native composition pressure: one large field, one decisive headline, and a small system cue instead of a report scaffold.
The cover should feel like the product already has a designed world before proof text appears.
composition_primitives: Reserve one canvas field for a dominant native object or SVG-like editable mark.
Place the headline against the field edge with an intentional asymmetry rather than a centered dashboard header.
typography_primitives: Use headline hierarchy with a clear 3x or larger size difference between title and supporting labels.
Keep support copy secondary and sparse so the headline owns the first read.
spacing_primitives: Use broad whitespace around the focal field and keep margin rhythm consistent across the sequence.
Do not fill the unused margin with badges, small metrics, or trace labels.
motion_or_sequence_primitives: Opening sequence starts with the field and headline, then reveals the small system cue after a pause.
Static PPT output must still trace the intended sequence as ordered native reveal steps.
module_implications: Generate a code module for a cinematic cover field and record the selected module in trace.
The module must use native editable PPT shapes, headline text, and trace metadata.
extraction_notes: Derived from source identity and keynote rhythm observations; not copied from source visuals.
The exact brand colors, marks, screenshots, and layouts are not copied.
do_not_copy: Do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, event graphics, or full prose.
Do not copy a reference cover composition one-to-one.
references.qa_probe: From the contact sheet, the cover should read as a designed product opening before any small explanatory text is readable.

visual_observations: A useful before/after slide needs native composition that visibly changes hierarchy, not a pair of equal cards with different labels.
The delta should be a visual event: one object becomes larger, clearer, or more editorially placed.
composition_primitives: Use the canvas for two unequal before/after fields with the after state visually dominant.
Add one delta marker that connects the old report state to the new designed state.
typography_primitives: Use headline hierarchy to separate state labels, delta label, and business implication.
Keep the implication line shorter than the two state labels combined.
spacing_primitives: Use whitespace to make the after state breathe and keep margin gutters larger than internal card gaps.
Avoid equal grid spacing when the point is hierarchy change.
motion_or_sequence_primitives: Sequence reveals the before state, then the delta marker, then the after state scale change.
The native static output must preserve that sequence in ordered trace steps.
module_implications: Generate a code module for editorial before/after delta with native thumbnails and a traceable hierarchy change.
The module must reject equal dashboard cards and record the selected visual component ids.
extraction_notes: Derived from public hierarchy and whitespace teaching plus commercial AI presentation observations; not copied from source visuals.
No source slide, screenshot, or brand system is embedded.
do_not_copy: Do not copy screenshots, source diagrams, course visuals, brand marks, or proprietary layouts.
Do not reproduce tutorial prose or exact slide examples.
references.qa_probe: From the contact sheet, a reviewer should see the before/after direction and the enlarged after-state object in under five seconds.

visual_observations: A product-system proof slide needs native composition that shows the product choosing an output, not a text description of the workflow.
The mini preview should look like a designed slide target with controls, density budget, and selected memory visible as small but readable primitives.
composition_primitives: Reserve the main canvas for a native mini-preview frame and place workflow controls around it as secondary objects.
Use one proof route line from data to memory to generated slide, with unequal emphasis on the generated slide target.
typography_primitives: Use headline hierarchy for the proof claim and small functional labels for controls.
Avoid paragraph copy inside the preview; labels must be short enough to remain readable.
spacing_primitives: Use whitespace between data, memory, and output zones; keep margin gutters quiet and consistent.
The mini-preview should not touch the slide edge or collapse into a tiny screenshot-like rectangle.
motion_or_sequence_primitives: Sequence builds from data anchor to selected memory to generated native slide preview.
If animation is unavailable, trace the intended build order and keep the static composition readable.
module_implications: Generate a code module for native system mini preview and proof route choreography.
The module must record production reference ids, aesthetic memory v2 ids, visual production module ids, fallback state, and trace evidence.
extraction_notes: Derived from presentation product and training observations; not copied from source layouts or training materials.
The output preview is an original native PPT abstraction, not a screenshot.
do_not_copy: Do not copy screenshots, product UI, deck templates, brand marks, or course exercise files.
Do not use a pasted interface capture as proof of native editability.
references.qa_probe: From the contact sheet, the proof slide should show a real product/system mini-preview rather than generic process cards.

visual_observations: The climax should be a native composition break: a larger object, darker or higher-contrast field, and fewer competing proof panels.
The close should hand off a decision gate instead of adding another report page.
composition_primitives: Give the climax object the largest canvas area in the deck and reduce surrounding proof surfaces.
Use a separate close canvas with one status, one handoff line, and a small editable mark.
typography_primitives: Use headline hierarchy where the climax verdict is short and secondary labels are restrained.
The closing line should read as governance and next action, not inspiration copy.
spacing_primitives: Use whitespace and margin discipline to make the climax object feel intentional, not oversized by accident.
Keep the close slide calm with wide margins and no appendix-like grid.
motion_or_sequence_primitives: Sequence scales the climax object after proof is established, then hands off to the release gate.
Trace must preserve scale emphasis and handoff order even when the generated PPT remains static.
module_implications: Generate code modules for climax hero object and release handoff gate.
The modules must include native fallback behavior, visual validation probes, and trace evidence.
extraction_notes: Derived from keynote rhythm and storytelling observations; not copied from video frames or webinar materials.
No audio, transcript, slide frame, or brand asset is stored.
do_not_copy: Do not copy screenshots, video frames, brand marks, keynote stage visuals, or webinar materials.
Do not copy source language or exact transition timing.
references.qa_probe: From the contact sheet, the climax should be the visual peak and the close should read as a decision handoff.
